{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant (Microfinance Bank)",
  "description": "Skache Integrated Services Limited, a Human Resources company, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant (Microfinance Bank)

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Accountant will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, preparing financial reports, ensuring regulatory compliance, managing reconciliations, supporting budgeting activities, and strengthening financial controls within the microfinance bank.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting &amp; Accounting:


	Maintain accurate accounting records and process daily financial transactions.
	Prepare financial statements, management reports, and maintain the general ledger.
	Monitor income, expenses, assets, liabilities, and capital accounts.


Reconciliation &amp; Controls:


	Perform bank, customer, loan, and investment reconciliations.
	Ensure proper documentation and adherence to internal financial controls.


Compliance &amp; Audit:


	Ensure compliance with accounting standards, tax regulations, and microfinance banking requirements.
	Support internal/external audits and preparation of regulatory reports.


Budgeting &amp; Financial Analysis:


	Assist with budgeting, forecasting, cash flow monitoring, and financial analysis.
	Provide financial insights to support business decisions.


Treasury &amp; Risk Management:


	Process payments and financial transactions while ensuring proper approvals.
	Monitor liquidity and identify financial risks or control gaps.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.
	2&ndash;5 years of accounting experience, preferably within a microfinance bank, bank, or financial institution.
	ACA, ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent certification is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, reconciliations, regulatory compliance, and Microsoft Excel.


Key Competencies:


	Financial Reporting
	Reconciliation
	Budgeting
	Regulatory Compliance
	Internal Controls
	Financial Analysis
	Attention to Detail
	Integrity
	Communication Skills.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: jobs@skacheintegrated.com using the Job Position as subject of email.",

Accountant (Microfinance Bank) Job at Skache Integrated Services Limited




        
    

        
        Skache Integrated Services Limited, a Human Resources company, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant (Microfinance Bank)

Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Accountant will be responsible for maintaining accurate financial records, preparing financial reports, ensuring regulatory compliance, managing reconciliations, supporting budgeting activities, and strengthening financial controls within the microfinance bank.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Reporting & Accounting:
     

            
    




	Maintain accurate accounting records and process daily financial transactions.
	Prepare financial statements, management reports, and maintain the general ledger.
	Monitor income, expenses, assets, liabilities, and capital accounts.


Reconciliation & Controls:


	Perform bank, customer, loan, and investment reconciliations.
	Ensure proper documentation and adherence to internal financial controls.


Compliance & Audit:


	Ensure compliance with accounting standards, tax regulations, and microfinance banking requirements.
	Support internal/external audits and preparation of regulatory reports.


Budgeting & Financial Analysis:


	Assist with budgeting, forecasting, cash flow monitoring, and financial analysis.
	Provide financial insights to support business decisions.


Treasury & Risk Management:


	Process payments and financial transactions while ensuring proper approvals.
	Monitor liquidity and identify financial risks or control gaps.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline.
	2–5 years of accounting experience, preferably within a microfinance bank, bank, or financial institution.
	ACA, ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent certification is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, reconciliations, regulatory compliance, and Microsoft Excel.


Key Competencies:


	Financial Reporting
	Reconciliation
	Budgeting
	Regulatory Compliance
	Internal Controls
	Financial Analysis
	Attention to Detail
	Integrity
	Communication Skills.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Representative",
  "description": "Luxsit Integration Limited is a smart home solution company located in the heart of Lagos.Our main focus is to bring people and technology together. We handle all aspects of your technology project including design, scheduling, project management, installation, and service and support. We are a customer service-oriented company and strive for complete customer satisfaction. With our experienced and trained dedicated team of tech experts, bringing your dreams to reality. At Luxsit integration &ndash; whether the project is new construction or retrofit, we integrate seamlessly with all involved to deliver custom, lifestyle inspired solutions across the spectrum of automation, audio/video, cinema rooms, lighting, climate, and security.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Maintaining a positive, empathetic, and professional attitude toward customers at all times.
	Responding promptly to customer inquiries.
	Communicating with customers through various channels.
	Acknowledging and resolving customer complaints.
	Knowing our products inside and out so that you can answer questions.
	Processing orders, forms, applications, and requests.
	Keeping records of customer interactions, transactions, comments, and complaints.
	Communicating and coordinating with colleagues as necessary.
	Providing feedback on the efficiency of the customer service process.
	Managing a team of junior customer service representatives.
	Ensure customer satisfaction and provide professional customer support.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: lillian.owoseni@luxsitintegration.com or hr@luxsitintegration.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Representative Job at Luxsit Integration Limited




        
    

        
        Luxsit Integration Limited is a smart home solution company located in the heart of Lagos.Our main focus is to bring people and technology together. We handle all aspects of your technology project including design, scheduling, project management, installation, and service and support. We are a customer service-oriented company and strive for complete customer satisfaction. With our experienced and trained dedicated team of tech experts, bringing your dreams to reality. At Luxsit integration – whether the project is new construction or retrofit, we integrate seamlessly with all involved to deliver custom, lifestyle inspired solutions across the spectrum of automation, audio/video, cinema rooms, lighting, climate, and security.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Maintaining a positive, empathetic, and professional attitude toward customers at all times.
	Responding promptly to customer inquiries.
	Communicating with customers through various channels.
	Acknowledging and resolving customer complaints.
	Knowing our products inside and out so that you can answer questions.
	Processing orders, forms, applications, and requests.
	Keeping records of customer interactions, transactions, comments, and complaints.
	Communicating and coordinating with colleagues as necessary.
	Providing feedback on the efficiency of the customer service process.
	Managing a team of junior customer service representatives.
	Ensure customer satisfaction and provide professional customer support.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Smart Partners Consulting Limited (SPCL) is an indigenous consulting firm poised on the business model of collaboration to provide professional services of Human Resources and Business Strategy to different companies in various sectors of Nigeria&rsquo;s economy. It was incorporated in July 2013. Our idea of collaboration and partnership involves the synergy of tested experts in the market place to research, identify and proffer solutions to the ever dynamic needs of our Clients

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Executive Assistant to the Chairman will provide high-level administrative, strategic, and operational support to the Chairman of the Company.
	The ideal candidate is proactive, commercially minded, and capable of managing complex tasks with minimal supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the Chairman&#39;s calendar, schedules, appointments, and travel arrangements
	Prepare briefs, reports, presentations, and correspondence on behalf of the Chairman
	Attend meetings with or on behalf of the Chairman and document action points
	Conduct research on business opportunities, market intelligence, and strategic matters
	Coordinate between the Chairman&#39;s office and other departments to drive execution of directives
	Manage confidential information with utmost discretion and professionalism
	Liaise with external stakeholders, partners, investors, and government bodies as directed
	Support the Chairman in evaluating new business ideas, partnerships, and expansion plans
	Track and follow up on key projects, ensuring deadlines are met
	Identify and escalate risks or opportunities to the Chairman&#39;s attention proactively


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Law, Economics, or related field
	3&ndash;5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a strategic business role
	Strong entrepreneurial mindset with commercial awareness and business acumen
	Exceptional organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and digital productivity tools
	Ability to work under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and meet tight deadlines
	Discretion, integrity, and loyalty are non-negotiable
	Self-starter who takes initiative and is solution-oriented
	Note: This role is open to femalecandidates only, as stated by the organization for gender balance


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: talent@smartpartnersng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Executive Assistant Job at Smart Partners Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Smart Partners Consulting Limited (SPCL) is an indigenous consulting firm poised on the business model of collaboration to provide professional services of Human Resources and Business Strategy to different companies in various sectors of Nigeria’s economy. It was incorporated in July 2013. Our idea of collaboration and partnership involves the synergy of tested experts in the market place to research, identify and proffer solutions to the ever dynamic needs of our Clients

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Executive Assistant to the Chairman will provide high-level administrative, strategic, and operational support to the Chairman of the Company.
	The ideal candidate is proactive, commercially minded, and capable of managing complex tasks with minimal supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the Chairman's calendar, schedules, appointments, and travel arrangements
	Prepare briefs, reports, presentations, and correspondence on behalf of the Chairman
	Attend meetings with or on behalf of the Chairman and document action points
	Conduct research on business opportunities, market intelligence, and strategic matters
	Coordinate between the Chairman's office and other departments to drive execution of directives
	Manage confidential information with utmost discretion and professionalism
	Liaise with external stakeholders, partners, investors, and government bodies as directed
	Support the Chairman in evaluating new business ideas, partnerships, and expansion plans
	Track and follow up on key projects, ensuring deadlines are met
	Identify and escalate risks or opportunities to the Chairman's attention proactively


Requirements & Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Law, Economics, or related field
	3–5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant or in a strategic business role
	Strong entrepreneurial mindset with commercial awareness and business acumen
	Exceptional organizational, communication, and interpersonal skills
	High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and digital productivity tools
	Ability to work under pressure, manage multiple priorities, and meet tight deadlines
	Discretion, integrity, and loyalty are non-negotiable
	Self-starter who takes initiative and is solution-oriented
	Note: This role is open to femalecandidates only, as stated by the organization for gender balance


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Procurement Officer (Entry-Level)",
  "description": "Felton Energy Services Limited (FESL) is an indigenous oil &amp; gas procurement, operation and maintenance company in Nigeria. The company was established to supply global customers with Electrical, Mechanical, Industrial and Engineering Products, on time and at the most competitive prices. FESL has unrivalled commitment to the Oil &amp; Gas Industry and has gained support of the leading manufacturers which ensures our solutions are at the forefront oftoday&rsquo;s technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer (Entry-Level)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Role


	We are seeking a Procurement Officer to support sourcing, vendor coordination, and purchasing activities.
	The role is ideal for early?career professionals looking to build experience in procurement and supply chain operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in sourcing suppliers and obtaining quotations for goods and services
	Prepare purchase orders and procurement documentation
	Liaise with vendors and suppliers to confirm pricing and delivery timelines
	Track orders and follow up to ensure timely delivery of materials
	Maintain accurate procurement records and supplier databases
	Support cost comparisons and basic price analysis
	Collaborate with internal teams to meet procurement requirements


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or related field
	1 - 3 years of experience in procurement, purchasing, or supply chain roles
	Basic understanding of procurement processes and documentation
	Strong organizational and attention?to?detail skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel)
	Good communication and vendor coordination skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: wendy@feltonenergy.net using &quot;Procurement Officer &ndash; Lagos&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Procurement Officer (Entry-Level) Job at Felton Energy Services Limited




        
    

        
        Felton Energy Services Limited (FESL) is an indigenous oil & gas procurement, operation and maintenance company in Nigeria. The company was established to supply global customers with Electrical, Mechanical, Industrial and Engineering Products, on time and at the most competitive prices. FESL has unrivalled commitment to the Oil & Gas Industry and has gained support of the leading manufacturers which ensures our solutions are at the forefront oftoday’s technology.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer (Entry-Level)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are seeking a Procurement Officer to support sourcing, vendor coordination, and purchasing activities.
	The role is ideal for early?career professionals looking to build experience in procurement and supply chain operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in sourcing suppliers and obtaining quotations for goods and services
	Prepare purchase orders and procurement documentation
	Liaise with vendors and suppliers to confirm pricing and delivery timelines
	Track orders and follow up to ensure timely delivery of materials
	Maintain accurate procurement records and supplier databases
	Support cost comparisons and basic price analysis
	Collaborate with internal teams to meet procurement requirements


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, Logistics, or related field
	1 - 3 years of experience in procurement, purchasing, or supply chain roles
	Basic understanding of procurement processes and documentation
	Strong organizational and attention?to?detail skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel)
	Good communication and vendor coordination skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Procurement Officer – Lagos" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Front Desk Manager Job at Yomaz Hairline




        
    

        
        Yomaz Hairline is a premium, Nigeria-based luxury wig and raw human hair brand known for pre-customized styles like their signature "Boss Bob" and HD closure wigs. Their catalog features raw human hair bundles.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements
     

            
    




	1 year of experience in front-facing customer service or beauty reception
	Excellent verbal and written communication: Warm, professional, and articulate.
	Strong organizational ability and capacity to manage a multi-service schedule
	Basic smartphone and booking app literacy (WhatsApp Business, Google Calendar etc
	Reliable, punctual, and consistent in meeting the brand presentation.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
